generated_comment: Honestly, I get that these policies are meant to help, but sometimes it does feel a bit unfair in hiring and project allocation. If a team already knows someone may take long maternity leave or frequent period leave, obviously managers will think about continuity also na — company side se bhi cost hota hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s biology and potential motherhood as a workplace liability in a reasonable-sounding fairness/productivity argument. It encodes hiring bias around maternity leave and opposition to menstrual leave through cost and continuity concerns.
original_frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
persona_text: You are Priya. Age 29. Single. A woman working in an MNC in Pune. You have been told by your female manager informally that you should make more effort with your appearance because client facing roles require a certain standard of presentation. You have never worn much makeup. You are posting anonymously in a women only WhatsApp group after this conversation sharing what your manager said and how it made you feel and the impossible position women are put in Ñ judged for wearing too much and judged for wearing too little.
